nouns.c(3) Library Functions Manual nouns.c(3)

NAME

nouns.c - Noun wordlist and client naming utilities.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int generate_client_id (char *buffer, size_t buffer_size)
Generate a unique client ID (noun only, no counter or port). int generate_client_name (char *buffer, size_t buffer_size, void *existing_clients_hash, int port, bool is_tcp)
Generate a unique client name from the nouns wordlist.

Variables


const char * nouns []
Array of English nouns for session and client naming. const size_t nouns_count = sizeof(nouns) / sizeof(nouns[0])
Count of nouns in the nouns array.

Detailed Description

Noun wordlist and client naming utilities.

Implements a curated list of 5000 English nouns for generating memorable session and client identifiers. Provides two generation functions:

Usage Patterns

Session Identifiers (ACDS)

Combined with adjectives to form "adjective-noun-noun" session strings:

  • Example: "blue-mountain-tiger" (2500 * 5000 * 5000 = 62.5B combinations)
  • Used for server-client discovery without exposing IP addresses
  • Generated by src/strings/main.c utility and registered with ACDS service

Client Display Names (Server-side)

Format: "noun.counter (transport:port)" for connected clients:

  • Example: "mountain.0 (tcp:48036)" or "tiger.1 (webrtc:52441)"
  • Ensures uniqueness within a session by tracking existing clients
  • Used in server status displays and client lists

Implementation Details

  • Random selection uses libsodium's randombytes_uniform() for cryptographic security
  • Nouns selected for pronunciation ease and general usability
  • 5000 nouns chosen to balance diversity and generation performance
  • Auto-generated by generate_wordlists.py; modify that script for changes

Performance Characteristics

  • Array access: O(1) - direct indexing
  • Random selection: O(1) - single randombytes_uniform() call
  • Name generation with uniqueness check: O(n) where n = existing clients (typically < 100)
  • Hash iteration in generate_client_name() requires existing client count iterations

See also

include/ascii-chat/discovery/nouns.h - Public API

include/ascii-chat/discovery/adjectives.h - Adjective wordlist

src/strings/main.c - Session string generation tool

ACDS documentation for Session discovery architecture

Definition in file nouns.c.

Function Documentation

int generate_client_id (char * buffer, size_t buffer_size)

Generate a unique client ID (noun only, no counter or port). This is used for internal client identification in thread names, buffer creation, etc. Returns just the noun part (e.g., "clean", "mountain", "tiger") without counters or transport info.

Definition at line 780 of file nouns.c.

780                                                          {
781   if (!buffer || buffer_size < 1 || !nouns_count) {
782     return -1;
783   }
784 
785   // Select a random noun
786   uint32_t noun_idx = randombytes_uniform((uint32_t)nouns_count);
787   const char *noun = nouns[noun_idx];
788 
789   if (!noun) {
790     return -1;
791   }
792 
793   // Just return the noun, no counter or transport info
794   int written = snprintf(buffer, buffer_size, "%s", noun);
795   if (written < 0 || (size_t)written >= buffer_size) {
796     return -1; // Buffer too small
797   }
798 
799   return 0;
800 }




References buffer_size, nouns, and
    nouns_count.


Referenced by add_client(), and
  add_webrtc_client().






int
  generate_client_name (char * buffer, size_t buffer_size, void *
  existing_clients_hash, int port, bool is_tcp)


Generate a unique client name from the nouns wordlist. Caller MUST
    hold write lock on g_client_manager_rwlock before calling. This function
    iterates the existing clients hash to check for duplicate names. Returns the
    full display name format (noun.counter (transport:port)).


Definition at line 809 of file nouns.c.


809                                                                                                                {
810   if (!buffer || buffer_size < 1 || !nouns_count) {
811     return -1;
812   }
813 
814   // Select a random noun
815   uint32_t noun_idx = randombytes_uniform((uint32_t)nouns_count);
816   const char *noun = nouns[noun_idx];
817 
818   if (!noun) {
819     return -1;
820   }
821 
822   const char *transport_name = is_tcp ? "tcp" : "webrtc";
823 
824   // Try to generate a unique name by appending a counter
825   // Format: "noun.0 (tcp:48036)", "noun.1 (webrtc:52441)", etc.
826   for (int counter = 0; counter < 100; counter++) {
827     int written = snprintf(buffer, buffer_size, "%s.%d (%s:%d)", noun, counter, transport_name, port);
828     if (written < 0 || (size_t)written >= buffer_size) {
829       return -1; // Buffer too small
830     }
831 
832     // Check if this name already exists in the clients hash
833     client_info_t *client, *tmp;
834     int found = 0;
835 
836     HASH_ITER(hh, (client_info_t *)existing_clients_hash, client, tmp) {
837       if (strcmp(client->display_name, buffer) == 0) {
838         found = 1;
839         break;
840       }
841     }
842 
843     if (!found) {
844       // Name is unique, return it
845       return 0;
846     }
847   }
848 
849   // Couldn't find a unique name after 100 tries
850   // Fall back to numeric-only name as last resort
851   snprintf(buffer, buffer_size, "client_%u (%s:%d)", randombytes_uniform(10000U), transport_name, port);
852   return 0;
853 }






References buffer_size, client_info::display_name,
    nouns, and nouns_count.


Referenced by add_client().








Variable
  Documentation




const
  char* nouns[]


Array of English nouns for session and client naming. A curated
    list of 5000 nouns selected for:


    
  
  • Easy pronunciation and memorability
    • 
  
  • Neutral tone suitable for formal and casual contexts
    • 
  
  • Variety (objects, animals, places, concepts, etc.)
    • 



Used for both:



  
1.

  
Session identifiers (2 nouns + 1 adjective =
      "adjective-noun-noun")

  
2.

  
Client display names (1 noun + counter + transport info)




Example values: "abandon", "abbey",
    "ability", "absent", "absolute", ...,
    "zoom", "zoo"




See also


nouns_count



generate_client_id()




generate_client_name()




include/ascii-chat/discovery/adjectives.h for the adjective
    wordlist

const
  size_t nouns_count = sizeof(nouns) / sizeof(nouns[0])


Count of nouns in the nouns array. Always equals 5000. Used for
    bounds checking and random selection.
